title: AmarShopping (Ponno Kinun)
summary: A full-featured multivendor e-commerce marketplace for the Bangladeshi market with 5 user roles, location-based delivery zones, real-time rider tracking, AI-powered admin chatbot with 6 provider adapters, and SSLCommerz payment integration — built from scratch (351 API endpoints, 58 tables, 47 React screens).

challenges.title: Guest-to-Customer Order Flow
challenges.problem: Users should browse and order without logging in, but orders must be linked if they later create an account
challenges.solution: resolve-guest middleware assigns a UUID guest token. When the guest upgrades (adds password), all their guest orders/cart merge into the new account

challenges.title: Real-Time Rider Location
challenges.problem: Admin needs to track rider positions on a map without polling
challenges.solution: Riders POST their lat/lng periodically; Leaflet map in admin panel renders markers that refresh via React Query

challenges.title: Delivery OTP Verification
challenges.problem: Must prove the customer received the package without expensive SMS gateways
challenges.solution: OTP generated at picked-up status, emailed to customer, rider enters it at delivery to confirm — if wrong, delivery fails

challenges.title: AI Chatbot SSE Streaming
challenges.problem: AI responses take 5-30 seconds — users need to see tokens as they arrive, not wait for full response
challenges.solution: Server-Sent Events (SSE) with ob_flush()/flush(). OpenRouter adapter reads the stream chunk-by-chunk and pushes each token via SSE. Other providers fall back to JSON. Fixed ob_start() buffer issues and timeout errors

challenges.title: Web Product Research Agent
challenges.problem: Admin needs to discover trending products from AliExpress/Amazon/Daraz and import them
challenges.solution: DuckDuckGo search → fetch article pages in parallel → strip HTML → send to LLM (GPT-4o-mini) for extraction → returns [{name, price, image}] → Add to Store button downloads image and creates product. 3-phase fallback: LLM → JSON-LD → deep fetch

challenges.title: Cross-Branch Order Forwarding
challenges.problem: An order destined for a district served by a different hub
challenges.solution: Hub managers can forward orders to another branch; the receiving hub then assigns the rider

challenges.title: 6 AI Providers in One Grid
challenges.problem: Each provider has different API formats, models, and auth
challenges.solution: Adapter pattern — AiProviderInterface with chat() and streamChat(), factory routes to the right adapter. Admin UI shows all 6 in a 2-column grid with per-provider config
